input.description: Create a Python 3 script named inventory_report.py. The script must read a JSON array of inventory records from a file passed with --input or from stdin when --input is omitted. Each record has sku, category, quantity, and price. The CLI must support --format text|json and --low-stock-threshold (default 5).
requirements: Use only the Python standard library.
Include a pure function build_report(records, low_stock_threshold=5).
The JSON output must include total_quantity, total_value, category_totals, and low_stock_skus.
Category totals must be sorted alphabetically by category name.
Round total_value to two decimal places.
Expose a main() entrypoint and guard it with if __name__ == '__main__'.
input.title: Inventory report CLI

sandbox.raw_output:
__MIRROR_NEURON_RESULT_START__
{"exit_code": 0, "stdout": "{
  "kind": "code_review",
  "round": 2,
  "task": {
    "description": "Create a Python 3 script named inventory_report.py. The script must read a JSON array of inventory records from a file passed with --input or from stdin when --input is omitted. Each record has sku, category, quantity, and price. The CLI must support --format text|json and --low-stock-threshold (default 5).",
    "requirements": [
      "Use only the Python standard library.",
      "Include a pure function build_report(records, low_stock_threshold=5).",
      "The JSON output must include total_quantity, total_value, category_totals, and low_stock_skus.",
      "Category totals must be sorted alphabetically by category name.",
      "low_stock_skus must be sorted alphabetically.",
      "Round total_value to two decimal places.",
      "Expose a main() entrypoint and guard it with if __name__ == '__main__'."
    ],
    "sample_records": [
      {
        "category": "books",
        "price": 12.5,
        "quantity": 3,
        "sku": "A-100"
      },
      {
        "category": "books",
        "price": 8.0,
        "quantity": 7,
        "sku": "B-200"
      },
      {
        "category": "games",
        "price": 59.99,
        "quantity": 2,
        "sku": "C-300"
      }
    ],
    "task_id": "inventory-report-cli",
    "title": "Inventory report CLI",
    "validation": {
      "expected": {
        "category_totals": [
          {
            "category": "books",
            "quantity": 10
          },
          {
            "category": "games",
            "quantity": 2
          }
        ],
        "low_stock_skus": [
          "A-100",
          "C-300"
        ],
        "total_quantity": 12,
        "total_value": 213.48
      },
      "low_stock_threshold": 4
    }
  },
  "file_name": "inventory_report.py",
  "candidate_code": "import argparse\nimport json\nimport sys\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Union\n\ndef build_report(records: List[Dict[str, Any]], low_stock_threshold: int = 5) -> Dict[str, Union[int, float, List[Dict[str, Any]], List[str]]]:\n    \"\"\"Builds an inventory report from a list of records.\"\"\"\n    total_quantity = 0\n    total_value = 0.0\n    category_totals: Dict[str, int] = {}\n    low_stock_skus: List[str] = []\n\n    for i, record in enumerate(records):\n        sku = record.get('sku')\n        category = record.get('category')\n        quantity = record.get('quantity', 0)\n        price = record.get('price', 0.0)\n\n        if sku is None or category is None:\n            print(f\"Warning: Skipping record {i+1} due to missing 'sku' or 'category'. Record: {record}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            continue\n\n        if not isinstance(quantity, int) or not isinstance(price, (int, float)):\n            print(f\"Warning: Skipping record {i+1} due to invalid 'quantity' or 'price' type. Record: {record}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            continue\n\n        total_quantity += quantity\n        total_value += quantity * price\n\n        if category not in category_totals:\n            category_totals[category] = 0\n        category_totals[category] += quantity\n\n        if quantity < low_stock_threshold:\n            low_stock_skus.append(sku)\n\n    sorted_category_totals = [\n        {\"category\": cat, \"quantity\": qty}\n        for cat, qty in sorted(category_totals.items())\n    ]\n\n    sorted_low_stock_skus = sorted(low_stock_skus)\n\n    return {\n        \"total_quantity\": total_quantity,\n        \"total_value\": round(total_value, 2),\n        \"category_totals\": sorted_category_totals,\n        \"low_stock_skus\": sorted_low_stock_skus\n    }\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Generate inventory reports.')\n    parser.add_argument('--input', help='Path to the input JSON file. Reads from stdin if omitted.')\n    parser.add_argument('--format', choices=['text', 'json'], default='text', help='Output format (text or json).')\n    parser.add_argument('--low-stock-threshold', type=int, default=5, help='Threshold for low stock items.')\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n    try:\n        if args.input:\n            with open(args.input, 'r') as f:\n                records = json.load(f)\n        else:\n            records = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        print(f\"Error: Input file '{args.input}' not found.\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except json.JSONDecodeError:\n        print(\"Error: Could not decode JSON from input.\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"An unexpected error occurred during input reading: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    if not isinstance(records, list):\n        print(\"Error: Input must be a JSON array.\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    report = build_report(records, args.low_stock_threshold)\n\n    if args.format == 'json':\n        print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))\n    else:\n        print(\"Inventory Report:\")\n        print(f\"Total Quantity: {report['total_quantity']}\")\n        print(f\"Total Value: ${report['total_value']:.2f}\")\n        print(\"\\nCategory Totals:\")\n        for item in report['category_totals']:\n            print(f\"  {item['category']}: {item['quantity']}\")\n        print(f\"\\nLow Stock SKUs (Threshold: < {args.low_stock_threshold}):\")\n        if report['low_stock_skus']:\n            for sku in report['low_stock_skus']:\n                print(f\"  - {sku}\")\n        else:\n            print(\"  None\")\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n",
  "summary": "The Python script successfully implements an inventory report CLI that reads from a file or stdin, supports JSON and text output formats, and calculates various inventory metrics.",
  "strengths": [
    "The code correctly implements the `build_report` pure function as required.",
    "Command-line argument parsing is handled robustly using `argparse`, including default values and input source selection.",
    "Error handling for file operations and JSON decoding is present and provides informative messages.",
    "The output formatting for both JSON and text modes adheres to the specified requirements, including sorting and rounding."
  ],
  "improvement_suggestions": [
    "Add validation for individual record fields (sku, category, quantity, price) within `build_report` to ensure they are of the expected types and not empty, rather than just printing warnings and skipping.",
    "Consider adding a more specific exception for invalid input data structure (e.g., not a list of dictionaries) after JSON decoding, rather than a generic `Exception` catch-all.",
    "The `build_report` function currently prints warnings to `sys.stderr` for malformed records. It would be more robust to return these warnings or errors as part of the report or raise exceptions.",
    "The `text` output format does not explicitly state the `low_stock_threshold` used in the report header, which could be confusing if the default is not used.",
    "The `build_report` function's type hint for `category_totals` is `Dict[str, int]`, but the return type hint is `Dict[str, Union[int, float, List[Dict[str, Any]], List[str]]]`. While technically correct, it could be more precise by defining a specific `Report` type or using a more specific union for the return dictionary's values."
  ],
  "risk_flags": [
    "The script does not handle cases where `quantity` or `price` might be negative, which could lead to unexpected `total_value` calculations.",
    "If the input JSON is extremely large, loading the entire structure into memory (`json.load`) could lead to `MemoryError`."
  ],
  "history": [
    {
      "stage": "codegen",
      "round": 1,
      "summary": "Creates a CLI tool to generate inventory reports from JSON data.",
      "changes": [

While technically correct, it could be more precise by defining a specific `Report` type or using a more specific union for the return dictionary's values."

  "code": "import argparse\nimport json\nimport sys\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Union\n\ndef build_report(records: List[Dict[str, Any]], low_stock_threshold: int = 5) -> Dict[str, Union[int, float, List[Dict[str, Any]], List[str]]]:\n    \"\"\"Builds an inventory report from a list of records.\"\"\"\n    if not isinstance(records, list):\n        raise ValueError(\"Input records must be a list.\")\n\n    total_quantity = 0\n    total_value = 0.0\n    category_totals: Dict[str, int] = {}\n    low_stock_skus: List[str] = []\n\n    for i, record in enumerate(records):\n        sku = record.get('sku')\n        category = record.get('category')\n        quantity = record.get('quantity')\n        price = record.get('price')\n\n        # Validate required fields and types\n        if sku is None or not isinstance(sku, str) or not sku:\n            print(f\"Warning: Skipping record {i+1} due to invalid or missing 'sku'. Record: {record}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            continue\n        if category is None or not isinstance(category, str) or not category:\n            print(f\"Warning: Skipping record {i+1} due to invalid or missing 'category'. Record: {record}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            continue\n        if quantity is None or not isinstance(quantity, int):\n            print(f\"Warning: Skipping record {i+1} due to invalid or missing 'quantity' (must be int). Record: {record}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            continue\n        if price is None or not isinstance(price, (int, float)):\n            print(f\"Warning: Skipping record {i+1} due to invalid or missing 'price' (must be number). Record: {record}\", file=sys.stderr)\n            continue\n\n        # Check for negative values\n        if quantity < 0:\n            print(f\"Warning: Record {i+1} has negative quantity ({quantity}). Treating as 0 for calculations.\", file=sys.stderr)\n            quantity = 0\n        if price < 0:\n            print(f\"Warning: Record {i+1} has negative price ({price}). Treating as 0 for calculations.\", file=sys.stderr)\n            price = 0.0\n\n        total_quantity += quantity\n        total_value += quantity * price\n\n        if category not in category_totals:\n            category_totals[category] = 0\n        category_totals[category] += quantity\n\n        if quantity < low_stock_threshold:\n            low_stock_skus.append(sku)\n\n    sorted_category_totals = [\n        {\"category\": cat, \"quantity\": qty}\n        for cat, qty in sorted(category_totals.items())\n    ]\n\n    sorted_low_stock_skus = sorted(low_stock_skus)\n\n    return {\n        \"total_quantity\": total_quantity,\n        \"total_value\": round(total_value, 2),\n        \"category_totals\": sorted_category_totals,\n        \"low_stock_skus\": sorted_low_stock_skus\n    }\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Generate inventory reports.')\n    parser.add_argument('--input', help='Path to the input JSON file. Reads from stdin if omitted.')\n    parser.add_argument('--format', choices=['text', 'json'], default='text', help='Output format (text or json).')\n    parser.add_argument('--low-stock-threshold', type=int, default=5, help='Threshold for low stock items.')\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    records: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n    try:\n        if args.input:\n            with open(args.input, 'r') as f:\n                records = json.load(f)\n        else:\n            records = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        print(f\"Error: Input file '{args.input}' not found.\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except json.JSONDecodeError:\n        print(\"Error: Could not decode JSON from input.\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"An unexpected error occurred during input reading: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    try:\n        report = build_report(records, args.low_stock_threshold)\n    except ValueError as e:\n        print(f\"Error processing input data: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        sys.exit(1)\n\n    if args.format == 'json':\n        print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))\n    else:\n        print(\"Inventory Report:\")\n        print(f\"Total Quantity: {report['total_quantity']}\")\n        print(f\"Total Value: ${report['total_value']:.2f}\")\n        print(f\"\\nCategory Totals:\")\n        for item in report['category_totals']:\n            print(f\"  {item['category']}: {item['quantity']}\")\n        print(f\"\\nLow Stock SKUs (Threshold: < {args.low_stock_threshold}):\")\n        if report['low_stock_skus']:\n            for sku in report['low_stock_skus']:\n                print(f\"  - {sku}\")\n        else:\n            print(\"  None\")\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    main()\n",
  "history": [
    {
